sections.paragraph:
ἱδρόω 1 aor. impv. ἱδρωσάτω (s. ἱδρώς; Hom. et al.; POxy 1242, 52; 4 Macc 3:8; 6:11) sweat, perspire fig. ἱδρωσάτω ἡ ἐλεημοσύνη σου εἰς τὰς χεῖράς σου let your alms sweat in your hands, i.e., do not give alms without due consideration D 1:6 (quot. of unknown orig.; cp. SibOr 2, 79; Mt 7:6.—S. ἐλεημοσύνη 2.).—DELG s.v. ἱδρώω.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

orig. orig. = original(ly)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 468
